expressions.english: warm-up exercise
lack of exercise
athlete
to do exercise; to work out
to like working out
Do you like working out?
What kind of exercise do you like?
Let's go to the park and work out together.
expressions.notes:
공원 = park
가다 = to go; to leave
같이 = together; with + N

expressions.english: I don't like exercising that much.
expressions.notes: 별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really

expressions.english: I did quite a lot of exercise, so I am tired.
expressions.notes:
많다 = to be a lot
많이 = is an adverb form of 많다.
피곤하다 = to be tired, exhausted; to be tiring

expressions.english: I listen to a lot of music while I work out.
expressions.notes:
음악 = music
들다 = to hear; to listen

expressions.korean: 살을 빼려면 굶지 말고 운동을 하세요.
expressions.english: In order to lose weight, don't skip meals, but exercise (instead).
expressions.notes:
살 = fat; flesh
빼다 = to subtract; to take something out
살을 빼다 = to lose weight
-（으）려면 = in order to + V
굶다 = to skip a meal; to starve

expressions.korean: 매일 저녁 헬스장에 가서 운동을 해요.
expressions.english: I go to the fitness center every evening and work out.
expressions.notes:
매일 = every day
저녁 = evening; dinner
헬스장 = gym

expressions.english: I exercised this morning and I feel very good.
expressions.notes:
아침 = morning; breakfast
-았/었/였더니 = since/because + I/we + V-ed
기분 = feelings; mood
상쾌하다 = to be refreshing

expressions.korean: 수영을 하기 전에는 준비 운동을 해야 돼요.
expressions.english: Before you swim, you need to warm up.
expressions.notes:
수영 = swimming
-기 전에 = before + S + V
-아/어/여야 되다 = to have to + V

expressions.korean: 요즘 살을 빼기 위해서 운동을 하고 있어요.
expressions.english: I am exercising these days in order to lose weight.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
-기 위해서 = in order to + V

expressions.korean: 저 사람이 제가 제일 좋아하는 운동 선수예요.
expressions.english: That person is my favorite athlete.
expressions.notes:
사람 = person, people
제일 = the best; the most

expressions.korean: 매일 이렇게 피곤한 건 운동이 부족해서인 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think the reason I am so tired every day is because of my lack of exercise.
expressions.notes:
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective
부족하다 = to be insufficient; to not have enough + N
- 것 같다 = it seems/looks like + S + V

expressions.korean: 의사 선생님이 건강하지려면 운동을 해야 한다고 했어요.
expressions.english: The doctor told me that I need to do some exercise in order to become healthy.
expressions.notes:
의사 = doctor
선생님 = teacher
의사 선생님 = a more polite way to address a doctor than 의사. When people address/call a doctor in person, people often call them 선생님 or 의사 선생님.
건강하다 = to be healthy
-（으）면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
-아/어/여야 하다 = should; have to; must
-다고 하다 = indirect ending - used to refer to what someone said

expressions.korean: 운동을 하는 건 안 좋아하지만, 스포츠 경기를 보는 건 좋아해요.
expressions.english: I don't like exercising, but I do like watching sports matches.
expressions.notes:
스포츠 = sport(s)
경기 = sport game, match
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read
